name: Fairy Faith Magic
description: Fairy Faith Magic, rooted in Celtic traditions, is a complex system of beliefs and practices centered around interaction with the 'Sidhe' or Fair Folk. It's born from a deep respect for nature, a belief in the permeability of the Otherworld, and the power of reciprocal relationships. Practitioners seek to maintain harmony with the fairy realm through offerings, rituals, and adherence to unspoken rules. Offenses against the Fair Folk, intentional or not, are believed to bring misfortune. This magic emphasizes respect, caution, and maintaining a delicate balance. It can involve divination, healing, protection, and influencing natural events. Fairy Faith Magic is not about commanding the Fair Folk but rather building respectful relationships and seeking their aid. It is a deeply embedded part of Celtic folklore and is often practiced in conjunction with other traditional Celtic beliefs. The land itself is considered sacred and intertwined with the fairy realm.
subtitle: Harmony with the Fair Folk

keyMyths.title: The Changeling
keyMyths.description: A fairy steals a human child, replacing it with a sickly fairy substitute. This myth highlights the dangers of disrupting the natural order and the importance of protection against fairy abduction.

sources.author: W.Y. Evans-Wentz
sources.description: A seminal work on the beliefs and practices surrounding fairies in Celtic cultures.
sources.text: A seminal work on the beliefs and practices surrounding fairies in Celtic cultures.
